mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches 10 percent, severe labor market deterioration forces immediate equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 10.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with declining housing starts indicate a shift from durable goods to shelter demand, signaling robust consumer resilience that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 5 percent while HOUST declines, yielding a negative out-of-sample Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, any month-over-month increase in the industrial production index confirms supply chain normalization, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of recovery-phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.5 percent monthly forces markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL rises by more than 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening and wage pressure, which compresses corporate margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 2-year windows during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery regime, quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2 percent annualized confirms economic stabilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2 percent fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of recovery regime observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month declines in industrial production reflect weakening aggregate demand and lower corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative during production declines.
